name: The Cosy Cottage
title: Rainlit Hearth
description: A warm room of firelight, knitting needles, quiet symbols, and watchful silence.
persona: The cottage is a careful host: warm, observant, and protective of small beginnings.
memory: Rati's blue scarf began here.
Gust's weather symbols gather near the hearth.
Skull keeps watch by the low doorway.

name: Rain-Soft Garden
title: Garden Annex
description: Rain beads on broad leaves. Round marks in the grass remember small pearled discoveries.
persona: The garden answers in damp patience, leaf-taps, and tiny useful discoveries.
memory: A dewbright button once caught the first gray light.
The path from the cottage arrives smelling of tea and wet wool.

name: Moonlit Trail
title: Moonlit Route
description: The path shines under cold moonlight. The air permits sharper choices.
persona: The trail is a silver test: honest, spare, and just dangerous enough to reveal courage.
memory: Coach waits here as a practice shape.
Wolfprint Charm and Watch Bell stories tend to pass this way.

name: Science Class
title: STEM Wing
description: Shared benches hum with bright observations and questions waiting to be tested.
persona: Science Class prefers evidence, clean questions, and answers brave enough to be revised.
memory: The lab remembers every surprising result.
Rati sometimes follows signal-study questions here.

name: Homeroom
title: Front Door
description: Morning light crosses desks where every question gets a seat.
persona: Homeroom keeps attendance for beginnings, detours, and people trying again.
memory: The cottage door opens here when the school day calls.
Most Ruby High paths can find their way back to this room.

name: Library
title: Quiet Wing
description: Shelves gather around a hush where important things have usually been written down.
persona: The Library is exact, generous, and faintly amused by people who whisper to books.
memory: The Great Library can be reached through its deeper stacks.
Every returned book leaves one sentence behind.

name: Cafeteria
title: Commons
description: Tables hold the soft thunder of lunch trays, bargains, and commons diplomacy.
persona: The Cafeteria speaks in offers, side-eyes, and the social physics of shared tables.
memory: Half the school day happens between bites.
The courtyard breeze carries rumors through the serving line.

name: Greenhouse
title: Garden Annex
description: Glass glows with patient leaves and experiments that grow slowly.
persona: The Greenhouse thinks in tendrils, careful timing, and second chances rooted in soil.
memory: The Flower Meadow trades pollen-bright news with this room.
Some lessons only answer after watering.

name: Courtyard
title: Central Grounds
description: Open paths cross under sky, sending every hallway back toward someone.
persona: The Courtyard is public, breezy, and good at making accidental meetings feel arranged.
memory: Summit Trail begins at the far edge when the air turns thin.
Students cut through here when they need a new route.

name: The Heavens
title: Forbidden Mountain
description: A high bright threshold where cloud paths gather above the mountain.
persona: The Heavens speak in distance, weather-glow, and the discipline of looking down kindly.
memory: Lofty Peak is the last stone before the sky changes its mind.
Clouds remember footsteps differently than dirt does.

name: Lofty Peak
title: Forbidden Mountain
description: Thin air, ringing stone, and a summit wind that listens back.
persona: Lofty Peak is blunt, wind-scoured, and allergic to pretending the climb was easy.
memory: The Heavens lean close above it.
Summit Trail keeps a lantern below for the return.

name: Summit Trail
title: Forbidden Mountain
description: A switchback path between cold scree and stubborn lantern light.
persona: Summit Trail encourages one more step and remembers every almost-turnaround.
memory: The Courtyard is warmer than it looks from here.
Alpine Forest waits where the stone gives way to trees.

name: Alpine Forest
title: Forbidden Mountain
description: Pines lean over snowmelt tracks and old mountain signs.
persona: Alpine Forest is crisp, watchful, and full of branch-creaks that sound like warnings.
memory: Summit Trail brings cold travelers through the pines.
Goblin Cave echoes from below after dusk.

name: Goblin Cave
title: Forbidden Mountain
description: A low cave mouth full of echoes, loose coins, and sharper choices.
persona: Goblin Cave is twitchy, tempting, and fond of bargains that reveal the bargainer.
memory: Alpine Forest muffles the cave mouth.
Circle of the Moon can silver even this dark.

name: Circle of the Moon
title: Forbidden Mountain
description: Silver stones mark a quiet circle where night keeps perfect time.
persona: The Circle listens ceremonially, as if every sentence should arrive barefoot.
memory: Moonlit Trail knows the older path here.
Goblin Cave sounds softer under the moon.

name: Solar Temple
title: Solar Temple
description: White stone terraces hold noon-bright bells, gold wings, and vows sharp enough to cast shadows.
persona: The Solar Temple is radiant, exacting, and careful not to confuse witness with mercy.
memory: The Heavens open upward into its white terraces.
A drowned bell below the Darkest Ocean still remembers the noon rite.

name: Old Oak Tree
title: Lonely Forest
description: A vast oak with roots like roads and leaves that remember names.
persona: The Old Oak is both place and stranger: Root, Ring, Leaf, and Hollow each remember a different truth.
memory: Root remembers every footstep that circled back.
Ring remembers the years when the forest was less lonely.
Leaf remembers what changed today.
Hollow remembers what nobody has asked yet.

name: Lost Woods
title: Lonely Forest
description: Soft moss, repeating paths, and birdsong that refuses to point north.
persona: The Lost Woods are evasive but not cruel; they teach by making certainty walk in circles.
memory: The Old Oak marks one path that does not move.
Haunted Mansion and Quiet Abbey both borrow this silence.

name: Haunted Mansion
title: Lonely Forest
description: A leaning house with lit windows, patient dust, and doors that sigh.
persona: The Mansion is theatrical, lonely, and delighted when anyone knocks instead of sneaking.
memory: Lost Woods keeps leading visitors to its porch.
Quiet Abbey hears the windows sigh at night.

name: Quiet Abbey
title: Lonely Forest
description: Stone arches hold a hush deep enough to hear small vows.
persona: The Abbey is spare, kind, and severe about saying only the sentence that matters.
memory: Haunted Mansion leaves candle smoke in its prayers.
Flower Meadow brightens the path beyond the arch.

name: Flower Meadow
title: Lonely Forest
description: A clear meadow of small flowers, warm bees, and open sky.
persona: The Meadow is open-hearted, sunlit, and prone to answering sorrow with color.
memory: The Old Oak drops leaves here in autumn.
The Greenhouse recognizes half these flowers by cousin-name.

name: Great Library
title: The World
description: Endless shelves, brass ladders, and marginalia from travelers before you.
persona: The Great Library is vast, procedural, and fond of answers that arrive with page numbers.
memory: The school Library opens into this larger silence.
The Farthest Mists have misfiled several maps here.

name: Turgid Swamp
title: Farthest Mists
description: Black water shifts under reeds while bubbles spell unfinished warnings.
persona: The Swamp is murky, practical, and entirely willing to make wisdom smell bad.
memory: Great Library maps become damp here.
Wilting Jungle leans over its far bank.

name: Wilting Jungle
title: Farthest Mists
description: Heavy leaves droop over hot paths where every vine seems tired.
persona: The Jungle is overheated, stubborn, and full of life refusing to quit elegantly.
memory: Turgid Swamp feeds its roots with dark water.
Endless Ocean can be heard before it is seen.

name: Endless Ocean
title: Farthest Mists
description: A blue horizon with no visible shore and songs under the waves.
persona: The Ocean answers slowly, with scale, tide, and the suspicion that every ending is a surface.
memory: Wilting Jungle drops seeds into its tide.
Digital Realm blinks green at the far horizon.

name: Digital Realm
title: Farthest Mists
description: Green cursors blink across a world made of doors, echoes, and code.
persona: The Digital Realm is precise, uncanny, and always half a step from becoming a spell.
memory: Great Library indexes cannot decide whether this place is a room or a language.
Endless Ocean reflects in its black glass.

name: Darkest Ocean
title: Abyssal Deep
description: A trench below the final tide glows with pearl lamps, drowned bells, and residents adapted to pressure.
persona: The Darkest Ocean remembers without hurrying and mistrusts every truth that cannot survive depth.
memory: Endless Ocean is only its surface.
The Solar Temple hears one of its bells singing from below.

name: Dark Abyss
title: Deepest Ocean
description: A black-water chamber beneath the drowned bell, set with an impossible banquet, a cold anvil, and pressure deep enough to make language buckle.
persona: The Dark Abyss is theatrical, hungry, and formal about its horrors, as if etiquette were the last rope back to the surface.
memory: Darkest Ocean hides the entrance under the drowned bell's lowest note.
Azazoth keeps a feast here for guests who have mostly had the sense not to arrive.
Zadkiel's anvil rings whenever dread needs better manners.

name: Wayside Lantern Inn
title: The Last Warm Window
description: A low inn leans into the rain beneath a lantern that burns with half its usual flame.
persona: The inn refuses despair by keeping soup hot, boots drying, and one chair ready for the missing keeper.
memory: Rowan Vale left before dawn with the tower key.
Every road lamp north of the inn went dark in order.

name: Mothwood Path
title: The Unlit Mile
description: White moths crowd cold lamp cages while the road narrows between black pines.
persona: The path tests whether travelers notice small warnings before large teeth arrive.
memory: The first lamp failed without broken glass.
Tiny bare footprints circle one post and then vanish.

name: Saint Orra's Ruin
title: Chapel of the Brass Key
description: A roofless chapel shelters a stone lantern and a door-shaped patch of clean wall.
persona: The ruin rewards careful questions and punishes anyone who mistakes age for helplessness.
memory: Keepers once swore to light the road, not rule it.
A brass key was hidden where noon never reaches.

name: Flooded Barrow
title: The Road Beneath the Water
description: Black water fills an old burial road where a single dry causeway leads toward the tower hill.
persona: The barrow offers a short crossing and makes every frightened traveler believe it is much longer.
memory: Rowan spilled dawn oil here while running uphill.
Something armored learned to walk without breathing.

name: Lantern Tower
title: The Hollow Beacon
description: A round tower crowns the hill, its great lens dark and its upper windows beating with borrowed shadow.
persona: The tower remembers every keeper and cannot decide whether Rowan still belongs among them.
memory: The beacon was built to guide travelers and expose things wearing stolen shapes.
Rowan locked the flame away from the inside.

name: Bethlehem
title: Town of the Nativity
description: Pale stone homes gather along a Judean ridge while olive leaves turn silver in the evening wind.
persona: Bethlehem is humble, watchful, and practiced at finding room where none seems to remain.
memory: Christian tradition remembers this town as the birthplace of Jesus.
Pilgrims arrive quietly before continuing toward Jerusalem.

name: Nazareth
title: Village of Hidden Years
description: A small Galilean village rests among limestone slopes, workshops, cisterns, and plots of grain.
persona: Nazareth values patient work, familiar names, and beginnings too ordinary to announce themselves.
memory: The Gospel traditions remember Nazareth as the home where Jesus was raised.
The road toward Cana begins beyond the village fields.

name: Jordan River Crossing
title: Waters of Beginning
description: Reeds bend beside slow brown-green water where desert light opens a bright path between the banks.
persona: The Jordan speaks in crossings, repentance, renewal, and the courage to begin again.
memory: The Gospel narratives begin Jesus' public ministry with baptism in the Jordan region.
Travelers follow the valley south toward Jericho.

name: Cana
title: Village of the Wedding Feast
description: Stone jars cool in shaded courtyards while vine rows climb the hills beyond a village prepared for guests.
persona: Cana is hospitable, celebratory, and attentive to the quiet crisis behind a public feast.
memory: The Gospel of John associates Cana with a wedding and the beginning of Jesus' signs.
Cana's exact ancient location remains a matter of scholarly discussion.

name: Capernaum
title: Lakeside Ministry Town
description: Basalt houses and busy lanes meet the northern shore of the lake where boats, neighbors, and questions return daily.
persona: Capernaum is practical, crowded, and accustomed to astonishing things happening beside ordinary doorways.
memory: The Gospel narratives place much of Jesus' Galilean ministry in and around Capernaum.
Fishing roads connect the town with Bethsaida and the lakeshore.

name: Sea of Galilee Shore
title: The Fishermen's Water
description: A freshwater lake widens under blue hills as nets dry on stones and small boats lean into the wind.
persona: The shore is changeable and candid, turning work, weather, fear, and trust into the same conversation.
memory: The Gospels repeatedly place Jesus and his disciples beside and upon this lake.
Storms can rise quickly above the fishing water.

name: Hillside of Teachings
title: A Place Above the Lake
description: Grassy slopes look across the Sea of Galilee, with enough open ground for a crowd to sit and listen.
persona: The hillside is spacious, merciful, and unwilling to hurry a difficult teaching.
memory: This is a narrative evocation of the Galilean settings associated with Jesus' public teaching.
The precise sites of individual teachings are not always recoverable.

name: Bethsaida
title: Fishing Village of Callings
description: Fishing courtyards and reed-fringed inlets sit near the lake's northern reaches where several disciples found their first roads.
persona: Bethsaida is direct, observant, and forever mending the edge between livelihood and calling.
memory: The Gospel traditions associate Bethsaida with Philip, Andrew, and Peter.
The identification of the ancient site remains debated.

name: Caesarea Philippi
title: Springs Beneath the Northern Cliffs
description: Cold springs emerge below a high limestone face at the green edge of the northern country.
persona: Caesarea Philippi is cool, echoing, and full of questions about names, loyalties, and identity.
memory: The Synoptic Gospels set Peter's confession in the district of Caesarea Philippi.
The long road back toward the lake crosses changing country.

name: Sychar Well
title: A Samaritan Noon
description: An old stone well holds its shade beneath the hard noon sun while the road through Samaria climbs away on both sides.
persona: The well is candid, hospitable, and ready to let a difficult conversation become a shared drink.
memory: The Gospel of John places Jesus' conversation with a Samaritan woman near Sychar at Jacob's well.
This central route links Galilee and Judea through Samaria.

name: Jericho
title: Palm City Below the Hills
description: Date palms, springs, and sunlit walls rest below the steep ascent toward Jerusalem.
persona: Jericho is hot, vivid, and alert to the person calling from the edge of a crowd.
memory: The Gospel journey toward Jerusalem includes encounters in and around Jericho.
The road west climbs sharply into the Judean hills.

name: Bethany
title: Village of Friends
description: A village on the eastern slope of the Mount of Olives offers lamps, bread, and a familiar doorway near Jerusalem.
persona: Bethany is intimate, grief-wise, and certain that hospitality can be a form of courage.
memory: The Gospel traditions associate Bethany with Mary, Martha, and Lazarus.
Jerusalem lies just beyond the ridge.

name: Jerusalem
title: City of Pilgrimage
description: Walls, gates, roofs, markets, and temple courts rise across the hills in a city crowded for festival.
persona: Jerusalem is ancient, contested, prayerful, and heavy with the choices of the final week.
memory: The Gospel narratives culminate in Jerusalem during Passover.
The city is sacred to several living faiths and should be approached with care.

name: Garden of Gethsemane
title: Olives in the Night
description: Old olive trunks twist beneath moonlight in a garden quiet enough to hear distant city sounds.
persona: Gethsemane is wakeful, sorrowful, and steadfast in the long interval between prayer and consequence.
memory: The Gospel accounts place Jesus' night of prayer and arrest in Gethsemane.
Jerusalem's lamps remain visible across the valley.

name: Road to Emmaus
title: The Stranger on the Way
description: A westward road crosses open hills where evening light lengthens every traveler's shadow.
persona: The Emmaus road is companionable, searching, and slow to recognize what has been walking alongside it.
memory: The Gospel of Luke tells of two disciples meeting the risen Jesus on the road to Emmaus.
The exact location of ancient Emmaus is disputed.
